questions: Should the agent message support multi-line/markdown formatting, or is plain text sufficient? The current design uses a single `// MESSAGE:` line which limits to one line of LLM output. Multi-line would need `// MESSAGE_START` / `// MESSAGE_END` delimiters.
For question-only responses, should the prompt input be cleared (current plan) or preserved so the user can see what they asked? Clearing matches the existing behavior for code generation.

Should edit-mode (existing code + edit instructions) also support questions, or only creation mode? E.g., user has generated an effect and asks 'what does the intensity parameter do?' — should this trigger a question response or always attempt code modification?

Is there a cost/latency concern with the single-call approach for triage? The alternative is a separate cheap classification call, but that adds ~2-5s latency. The single-call approach occasionally wastes a full generation call if the LLM misclassifies a question as a code request.

success_criteria.criterion: Edge function returns a `message` string field when LLM includes a // MESSAGE: line in code generation responses
Edge function returns `isQuestionResponse: true` with message and no code when user input is a question
Question-only responses do not trigger code validation errors or retry logic
EffectCreatorPanel displays the agent message inline when present
Agent message is dismissible and clears on new generation or form reset
Question-only responses do not overwrite existing code/name/schema state in the panel
Existing effect generation flow (create, edit, retry) continues to work unchanged when no MESSAGE line is present
Message display is visually distinct but not intrusive — subtle styling consistent with the panel's existing design language
LLM reliably distinguishes questions from effect requests (>90% accuracy on obvious cases)
No new files created — all changes are edits to existing files
Edge function response size does not increase significantly for normal code generation (message is 1-2 sentences)
Manual smoke tests: generate effect with message, ask a question, edit existing effect with message, dismiss message, verify retry still works

assumptions: Plain text single-line messages are sufficient for v1 — can extend to multi-line later if needed
Question detection is handled by the LLM via prompt instructions (no separate classifier call) to avoid additional latency
The agent message replaces the existing 'Effect Description' display for code responses (since the message is more useful and the description is already captured in metadata)
Question-only responses should work in both creation and edit modes — if a user asks a question while editing, they get an answer without losing their existing code
No persistence of messages beyond the current dialog session — messages are ephemeral state
The dismiss button uses the X (close) icon from lucide-react which is already available in the project
